step.title: Fill Content Templates
step.goal: Work through each selected content template, filling every section with quality content through discussion with the user. The templates are self-documenting — each FILL comment teaches what goes there.
step.note: This is the craft step. Don't rush. Each template section deserves proper attention. The templates contain embedded fill instructions — read them carefully and use them to guide the conversation.

substep.title: Process Each Template
substep.action:
For each per-agent file in the agent's includes list (see Step 2):
1. READ the matching template from references/templates/{template_name}.md 2. IDENTIFY all sections marked with FILL comments 3. For each FILL section: a. Read the embedded fill instructions (examples, rules, tips) b. Present what this section needs to the user c. Discuss — ask questions, explore options, iterate d. Fill the section with quality content 4. PRESENT the completed file for review 5. WRITE the filled file to .vera/agents/{agent_id}/{file_name} 6. Move to the next file
Mapping templates → per-agent files in dubstack: - persona.md template → .vera/agents/{agent_id}/persona.md (primary AND subagent) - menu.md template → .vera/agents/{agent_id}/menu.md (primary only) - subagent-instructions.md template → .vera/agents/{agent_id}/instructions.md (subagent only)
Note: frontmatter.md is filled in Step 3 (it's the OpenCode YAML frontmatter, not a content template).

substep.title: Quality Standards
substep.action:
Apply these quality standards to every fill operation:
- Don't accept vague answers — the template shows what quality looks like - Push back on generic content: "Professional and helpful" → ask for metaphors - Push back on rule-list drift in persona principles (see persona.md template's bad-example callout) — principles describe how the agent THINKS, not what it must DO - Check consistency: skills in frontmatter.md permission map should match menu items in menu.md (primary) - For subagents: <input-expectations> must be specific enough for the dispatching agent to pack correctly; <workflow> output format must be parseable by the dispatching agent - For primary agents: menu must cover every skill identified in Step 1 - For subagent instructions.md: capability boundaries live in persona <principles> (stance), frontmatter permissions (enforcement), or workflow step content (operational)

substep.title: Per-Template Checkpoint
substep.action:
After filling each template, present the result and ask:
[a] Refine this template [c] Continue to next template [y] YOLO remaining

section.title: Reading Template Fill Instructions
section.description:
Each content template has XML comments with the pattern: <!-- FILL: field_name ... -->
These comments contain: - What goes in this field - Format requirements - Good and bad examples - Tips for primary vs subagent variants
The instructions are the template's own documentation. Read them carefully — they contain the domain knowledge for each section.

rules.rule: Start with the template's embedded examples to anchor the conversation
Ask one section at a time — don't overwhelm with all fields at once
If the user gives a short answer, show them the template's "Good" examples
If unsure, propose a draft and ask "Does this capture it?"

Both primary AND subagent personas: push for real professional archetype priors activation; reject functional labels ("Web Research Specialist") in favor of archetype labels ("Senior Research Analyst + Multi-Source Verification Specialist")
Both primary AND subagent personas: push for vivid imagery or archetype-anchored disposition in communication_style; reject generic descriptors
Subagent instructions.md: push for precise <input> attributes (type, required, values) and parseable <workflow> output format

section.title: Writing Filled Templates
section.description: When writing the filled template to the agent directory: - REMOVE all FILL instruction comments (they were for creation guidance) - KEEP the XML structure (tags, nesting) - REPLACE comment placeholders with actual content - The result should be a clean, production-ready file
section.example:
Template (in references/templates/): <role> <!-- FILL: role ... --> </role>
Filled (in .vera/agents/{agent_id}/persona.md): <role>Strategic Business Analyst + Requirements Expert</role>

rules.rule: Skills in menu.md items must match skills in frontmatter.md permission.skill map (primary only)
Subagent <input-expectations> in instructions.md must match what the dispatching agent actually packs into the dispatch prompt
Subagent <workflow> output format must be parseable by the dispatching agent — strict JSON schemas require strict emission

Persona <principles> carry capability boundaries as positive stance (subagents); workflow step content carries operational discipline; permission map carries hard prohibitions — verify these three layers don't contradict
Persona principles for BOTH primary AND subagent must be mental-posture prose, not rule lists — see persona.md template's bad-example callout
Subagent persona <role> uses the same real-archetype + delimiter rule as primary (e.g., "Senior Code Reviewer + Multi-Angle Verification Specialist")
